# altinity_instance_types (Data Source)

List the instance types available for a cloud provider in a region (GET /cloud/options?platform=<cloud_provider>&region=<region>&type=*). Use an instance type's name as an altinity_node_type `code`, and its cpu/memory as the node type's cpu/memory.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# List the instance types available for a cloud provider in a region. Use an
# instance type's name as an altinity_node_type `code`.
data "altinity_instance_types" "gcp" {
  cloud_provider = "gcp"
  region         = "us-east1"
}

output "instance_type_names" {
  value = [for t in data.altinity_instance_types.gcp.instance_types : t.name]
}

output "zones" {
  value = data.altinity_instance_types.gcp.zones
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `cloud_provider` (String) Cloud provider (e.g. gcp, aws).
- `region` (String) Region code (see the altinity_regions data source).

### Read-Only

- `instance_types` (Attributes List) The available instance types. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--instance_types))
- `zones` (List of String) Availability zones in the region.

<a id="nestedatt--instance_types"></a>
### Nested Schema for `instance_types`

Read-Only:

- `cpu` (Number) vCPUs.
- `cpu_allocatable` (Number) Allocatable vCPUs (after system overhead).
- `memory` (Number) Memory in GiB.
- `memory_allocatable` (Number) Allocatable memory in GiB.
- `name` (String) Instance type name (use as a node type code).
